title: 10 best herbal creams for neuritis

remedy_properties: Ginkgo biloba: Ginkgo biloba is believed to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation, which may help alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as numbness, tingling, and pain.
St. John's Wort (Hypericum perforatum): St. John's Wort has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce inflammation and relieve pain associated with neuritis.
Turmeric/Curcumin: Turmeric contains curcumin, a compound with potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba, also known as maidenhair tree, creams helps with neuritis because it improves blood circulation and reduces inflammation in the nerves. The active compounds in Ginkgo biloba, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help to protect and repair damaged nerve cells. By enhancing oxygen delivery and reducing oxidative stress, Ginkgo biloba creams promote nerve health and alleviate symptoms of neuritis, including numbness, tingling, and pain, allowing for faster recovery and improved nerve function.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the nerves, alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Ginkgo flavone glycosides (including quercetin and isorhamnetin): These flavonoid compounds have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help protect nerve tissue and reduce neuritis symptoms.
Terpenoids (including bilobalide): These compounds have neuroprotective effects, which may help reduce nerve damage and inflammation associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the most commonly used part due to their high flavonoid and terpene content, which contribute to their medicinal properties.
Seeds: Ginkgo seeds are used for their oil, which has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, helping to alleviate neuritis symptoms.
Barks: The bark of the Ginkgo biloba tree is also used, possibly due to its potential anti-inflammatory properties and ability to improve blood circulation.
remedy_recipe: Extract ginkgo biloba leaves by steeping 10g of dried leaves in 100ml of jojoba oil for 2 hours.
Filter the mixture through a cheesecloth to remove any solids and retain the infused oil.
Melt 50g of beeswax in a double boiler at a temperature of 180-200°f for 10 minutes.
Combine the melted beeswax with 20g of shea butter and 10ml of the infused ginkgo biloba oil.
Pour the mixture into a mold and let it cool and solidify for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the nerves, which can contribute to neuritis symptoms such as numbness, tingling, and burning pain.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms like pain, stiffness, and limited mobility.

Pain-relieving properties: St John's Wort has been shown to have analgesic and anxiolytic effects, which can help alleviate chronic pain associated with neuritis, such as sciatica or shingles-related pain.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hypericum perforatum, also known as St John's Wort, creams helps with neuritis because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compounds in Hypericum, such as hyperforin and hypericin, have been shown to reduce inflammation and promote healing in damaged nerve tissue. By reducing pain and inflammation, Hypericum perforatum creams can provide relief from neuritis symptoms, allowing for improved nerve function and reduced discomfort. This natural remedy offers a potential alternative for managing neuritis symptoms.
remedy_constituents: Hyperforin: This terpenoid compound has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, helping to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress associated with neuritis.
Quercetin: As a flavonoid phenolic compound, quercetin has anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which can help alleviate pain and inflammation in neuritis.

Nortriterpenoids (especially Amentoflavone): These compounds have been found to have potent anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and neuroprotective effects, which can help reduce inflammation and promote nerve health in neuritis.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in hyperforin and hypericin, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them suitable for creams treating neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers contain hyperforin and hypericin, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, useful in creams for neuritis treatment.
Leaves: They are also a source of flavonoids, such as quercetin, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, beneficial for treating neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried hypericum perforatum flowers from a known source and clean them thoroughly.
Mix the dried hypericum perforatum flowers with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture under low heat for 10-15 minutes or until the beeswax is fully dissolved and the mixture is smooth.
Add 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil and 5 milliliters of aloe vera gel to the mixture and stir well.
Remove the mixture from the heat and allow it to cool and thicken completely before transferring it to a container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Turmeric creams containing curcumin, a compound found in turmeric, have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with neuritis.
Antioxidant property: The antioxidant properties of turmeric creams can help protect against oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to the development of neuritis.
Pain-relieving property: Curcumin in turmeric creams has been shown to have analgesic effects, reducing pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa, also known as turmeric, creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The active compound curcumin in turmeric has been shown to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nervous system, which are key factors in the development of neuritis. By modulating the immune response and promoting neuronal health, turmeric creams can help alleviate symptoms such as pain, numbness, and tingling associated with neuritis, providing natural relief and promoting healing in the affected nerves.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): DMC, a curcuminoid, helps with neuritis by inhibiting pro-inflammatory enzymes and reducing the production of inflammatory cytokines, thereby alleviating neuritis symptoms.

Volitin (Caryophyllene oxide): Volitin, a sesquiterpene, helps with neuritis by exerting anti-inflammatory and neuroprotective effects, which may help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

remedy_recipe: Grate 1-2 tsp of dried curcuma longa root into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar.
Combine the powder with 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat.
Stir the mixture constantly for 5-7 minutes or until the curcuma longa is fully dissolved.
Add 1/4 tsp of beeswax and stir until melted and fully incorporated into the mixture.
Remove the double boiler from heat and let the cream cool and thicken for 30-40 minutes before applying.

remedy_recipe: Extract 100g of dried panax ginseng roots and soak them in 500ml of water for 2 hours.
Combine the soaked ginseng extract with 500ml of distilled water and 20g of glycerin.
Heat the mixture to 80°c for 30 minutes and then let it cool.
Mix 10g of beeswax and 20g of shea butter with 50g of the cooled ginseng mixture.
Blend the mixture until it thickens and pour it into a container for storage.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers at peak bloom and dry them properly.
Combine 50 grams of dried lavandula angustifolia flowers with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for two to three weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture and discard the solids, then filter the liquid through a coffee filter.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 20 milliliters of the infused oil and 20 milliliters of distilled water.

Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter to remove the roots and sediment.
Mix 100g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter in a double boiler to melt the wax and butter.

Strain the infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl and discard the solids.
Combine 1 cup of coconut oil and 1/2 cup of beeswax in a double boiler.
Add the infused calendula liquid to the coconut oil mixture and stir until fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from your garden or local market in the early morning hours.

Mix 10g of grated beeswax with 20g of coconut oil and 10g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Add the powdered peppermint to the melted wax-oil mixture and stir until well combined, allowing it to cool and thicken for at least 30 minutes.

remedy_recipe: Collect high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers in amounts of 20g for every 100ml of cream base mixture.
Grind the dried matricaria chamomilla flowers into fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground matricaria chamomilla powder with distilled water at a ratio of 1:10 to form a paste, allowing it to steep for 30 minutes.
Strain the paste through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused liquid.
Combine the infused liquid with a cream base mixture consisting of 100ml distilled water, 50ml cetyl alcohol, and 10g beeswax, heating the mixture gently until the ingredients are fully incorporated.

remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry vitis vinifera leaves in a warm place for at least two weeks.
Grind the dried vitis vinifera leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Mix one teaspoon of the powdered vitis vinifera leaves with two tablespoons of coconut oil in a small bowl.
Add three drops of peppermint essential oil to the mixture and stir well for five minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken at room temperature for 30 minutes before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Kansas coneflower cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like salicylic acid, which helps reduce swelling and pain associated with neuritis in the nerves.

Antioxidant properties: The cream's antioxidant properties, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, help neutralize free radicals that can damage nerve tissue, thereby alleviating symptoms of neuritis.

Pain-relieving properties: Kansas coneflower contains analgesic compounds like ursolic acid, which helps reduce pain and discomfort associated with neuritis by blocking the production of pain-causing chemicals in the body.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce pain and inflammation associated with neuritis.

Barks: The barks of the plant have been traditionally used to treat neuralgia, which is a type of pain or burning sensation that occurs along nerve pathways, making them suitable for medicinal creams for neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Gather echinacea angustifolia dried root extract of 10% concentration.
Combine the extracted powder with distilled water in a double boiler setup.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes at 180-200°f temperature and 15-20 pounds pressure.
Mix the cooled extract with beeswax and coconut oil in a ratio of 1:2:1 by weight.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and allow it to cool and solidify within 24 hours.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with neuritis because of their unique blend of anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The herb, Echinacea purpurea, contains compounds that target inflammation in the nerves, reducing pain and discomfort associated with conditions like sciatica and peripheral neuritis. Additionally, the cream's emollient properties soothe and calm irritated nerve endings, promoting relaxation and reducing muscle spasms. By addressing these underlying issues, Kansas coneflower creams provide effective relief from neuritic symptoms, allowing individuals to regain mobility and comfort in their daily lives.

Feverfew creams helps with neuritis because they contain parthenolide, a compound that reduces inflammation and relaxes muscles. When applied topically, feverfew cream can soothe irritated nerves and ease discomfort caused by conditions such as trigeminal neuralgia and sciatica. The anti-inflammatory properties of feverfew also help to reduce swelling and pain in the affected area, providing quick relief from symptoms of neuritis. Additionally, feverfew's natural antispasmodic effects can help to calm muscle spasms and cramping that often accompany neuritis.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's active compound parthenolide has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, which can help reduce pain and swelling associated with neuritis.

Antioxidant activity: Feverfew's antioxidant properties can help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, which is thought to contribute to the development of neuritis.

Spasmolytic activity: Feverfew has been shown to have spasmolytic properties, meaning it can help relax muscle spasms and reduce inflammation in the nerves, which can provide relief from the pain and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Feverfew roots are known for their anti-inflammatory properties, which help soothe and relieve nerve pain associated with neuritis.
Flowers: The flowers of the feverfew plant contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried flowers of tanacetum parthenium, measuring out one part by weight for every four parts of carrier oil.
Combine the dried flowers with carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil in a ratio of 1:4 by weight.
Steep the mixture for 14 days in a cool dark place, shaking occasionally, to allow for active compounds extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean container, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Add beeswax and vitamin e oil to the infused oil at a ratio of 10% by weight, stirring until fully incorporated and whipped.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with neuritis because they contain potent anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort in affected nerves. The creams' natural soothing properties calm irritated nerve endings, promoting healthy tissue regeneration and repair. Additionally, hops' neuroprotective agents help to shield nerves from further damage, hastening the recovery process. By targeting inflammation and promoting nerve health, herbal hops creams offer a safe and effective way to manage neuritis symptoms, providing relief for those suffering from this often debilitating condition.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Herbal hops creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like humulones and lupulones, which help reduce swelling and pain in affected nerves, providing relief from neuritis symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: The antioxidants present in herbal hops creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, help protect the nervous system from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthy nerve function and reducing inflammation.

Anxiolytic and sedative properties: Herbal hops creams also contain anxiolytic (anxiety-reducing) and sedative compounds like linalool and myrcene, which can help alleviate anxiety and promote relaxation, which is often disrupted in individuals experiencing neuritis.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties, making them effective in relieving neuralgia and neuritis pain.
Buds: Containing flavonoids and terpenoids, buds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that can help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with neuritis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh humulus lupulus leaves in early morning mist and dry them for 2-3 hours under shade.
Grind the dried leaves into a fine powder using mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine the powdered humulus lupulus with 20% beeswax, 30% coconut oil, and 50% sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30-40 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Remove from heat and let cool to room temperature before transferring it to glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon balm creams helps with neuritis because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which effectively reduce swelling and pain in the nerves. The cream's ability to ease inflammation also diminishes pressure on the nerve endings, allowing them to function properly again. Additionally, lemon balm's soothing and calming effects help to calm irritated nerves, promoting relaxation and reducing tension that can exacerbate neuritis symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Lemon balm's antioxidant properties help to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the nerves, which can alleviate symptoms of neuritis such as pain and numbness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemon balm have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce swelling and ease nerve compression, providing relief from neuritis-related discomfort.
Sedative and antispasmodic effects: Lemon balm's calming and relaxing properties can help to reduce muscle spasms and cramping associated with neuritis, promoting a sense of calm and relaxation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and pain associated with neuritis.
Leaves: Contain rosmarinic acid, a potent antioxidant that soothes nerve irritation and reduces swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh melissa officinalis leaves at dawn for maximum potency and quality.
Dry the harvested leaves using air circulation or low-temperature dehydrators within 24 hours.
Combine 20 grams of dried melissa officinalis leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a warm, dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to facilitate infusion.
Strain and filter the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter within 30 minutes of preparation time.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for neuritis are topical preparations that combine natural herbs with moisturizing properties to alleviate symptoms of nerve pain and inflammation. These creams, often containing ingredients like arnica, capsaicin, and St. John's Wort, help reduce pain, itching, and numbness associated with neuritis. Examples of such creams include those with peppermint, ginger, and aloe vera, which can improve quality of life by reducing discomfort and promoting relaxation. They offer a natural alternative for managing neuritis symptoms.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to neuritis that are treated with herbal creams are conditions such as neuralgia, sciatica, and rheumatoid arthritis. These herbal creams typically contain a combination of anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving herbs like arnica, devil's claw, and capsaicin, which help to reduce inflammation, ease muscle tension, and alleviate chronic pain.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with neuritis is a blend of arnica, calendula, and St. John's Wort. Arnica cream reduces inflammation and swelling, while calendula cream promotes wound healing and soothes the skin. St. John's Wort cream, rich in hyperforin, has anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help repair damaged nerve tissue. Together, these creams create a powerful synergy that can help alleviate neuritis symptoms, such as pain, numbness, and tingling, promoting faster recovery and reducing discomfort.
